title: Regarding 4th Impacts Cancellation
wikitext:
[]: Regarding 4th Impact's Cancellation             Quote Post by Sherbert » Sat Jun 29, 2013 2:07 am It seems obvious that with Shinji being an "Impact Trigger" and being important to Neo-NERV, Misato would know that Shinji was piloting Unit 13 and therefore being the cause of the beginning of the 4th Impact. This would have most likely caused some anger and stress to Misato and the rest of the WILLE crew at first, but what came after should have also had a profound effect on Misato and maybe, the other WILLE members. Right after the AAA Wunder was freed from the Mark 9's control, the Wunder regained visual feed on what was going on outside. Without a fully recovered engine as Ritsuko put it, the WILLE crew were rendered useless for the time being and all eyes would have obviously been on Unit 13 to see what would happen next. What they would have witnessed was Unit 13 impale itself with the Spears of Longinous, almost as if, in the minds of the WILLE crew, Shinji realized the mess he was creating and decided to sacrifice himself along with the Unit in order to prevent 4th Impact. A couple of minutes later after the ordeal, Ritsuko says. "I don't know who did it, but the 4th Impact was stopped", followed by this statement were shown a close-up of a very stressed and regretful looking Misato. In their minds, it could have been Shinji who stopped 4th Impact himself. WILLE's and Misato's view on Shinji might have been changed at that very moment. For all they know, Shinji could be dead. Misato's expression certainly expressed that look. Any thoughts on this? It might be a little far-fetched, maybe not what Anno intended.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Jun 29, 2013 3:44 am I like that thought, though I will want to go back and view again with it in mind. But if so, then when they discover in Final that Shinji is still alive, and that he did not do this redeeming thing, won't their anger against him be not merely renewed, but intensified?

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Sat Jun 29, 2013 3:48 am I think they know that Shinji is still alive, Mari told them maybe?

[]:             Quote Post by Sherbert » Sat Jun 29, 2013 10:54 am pwhodges wrote: I like that thought, though I will want to go back and view again with it in mind. But if so, then when they discover in Final that Shinji is still alive, and that he did not do this redeeming thing, won't their anger against him be not merely renewed, but intensified?

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sat Jun 29, 2013 1:05 pm Most likely Mari will tell them about Eva-13, as well as the fact that she stopped the Impact by pulling out Shinji's entry plug. If Shinji is taken back to the Wunder they'll probably want an explanation out of him, as well.

[]:             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Sat Jun 29, 2013 6:34 pm Ritsuko tells Misato to use the Choker because he still has the potential to be a trigger, they knew NERV was going to start another Impact, however as Kaworu states, because he became the 13th angel, he was the trigger, not Shinji. That being said, who's to say that Rei 2.0 didn't become the 10th angel and was an Impact trigger as well? Also, "he still has the potential to be a trigger", either she means he was already one before, or he wasn't but still has the potential to become one, which is it?

[]:             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Sat Jun 29, 2013 6:41 pm Kaworu may talk about that the choker had activated because he was detected as an angel (it was made for him originally, and Kaworu was the trigger of its activation), and there is no Shinji's guilt in Kaworu's death. Shinji actually may be tje trigger of awakening, because Kaworu's death created powerful emotional burst in him which prevented hibernation of 13 with the spears, so the door remained open.
